claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this! I have one child I'd like to ask about - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have a son named Alex. 

To help assess whether Alex is eligible for Child Benefit, I need to gather some details about his circumstances.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful information.

**Is Alex still in full-time education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that's useful to know.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask one more clarifying question to ensure I have the full picture:

**What is your current residency status in the UK? Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status (such as Indefinite Leave to Remain)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen living here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for clarifying that. Now let me read the official guidance to ensure I assess Alex's eligibility correctly based on the current rules.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! I've reviewed the official Child Benefit eligibility guidance. Based on the information you've provided about Alex, I can now give you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** You are **eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

- **Age**: Alex is 17 years old and under 20
- **Education**: He's in full-time approved education (A-levels at college)
- **Living arrangements**: He lives with you
- **Residency**: You're a British citizen living in the UK

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex meets all these criteria, so you should be able to claim. If you haven't already done so, you can contact HMRC to make a claim for him.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and in full-time education (A-levels at college). According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. A-level courses are considered approved education. Additionally, Alex lives with you and you are a British citizen living in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. You are therefore responsible for Alex and meet all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for your son Alex. He meets all the key eligibility criteria: he is 17 years old and under 20, he is in approved full-time education (A-levels), he lives with you, and you are a British citizen resident in the UK. As the person responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, you can claim Child Benefit for him."]

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. A-level courses are considered approved education. Additionally, Alex lives with you and you are a British citizen living in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. You are therefore responsible for Alex and meet all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
